title: Le Petit Prince Hong Kong: Naturalistic fMRI and EEG dataset from older Cantonese speakers
description: This multimodal neuroimaging dataset comprises task-based and resting-state fMRI, structural MRI, and EEG recordings from 52 healthy older Cantonese speakers (mean age 69 years) listening to naturalistic speech from 'The Little Prince' in Cantonese. The dataset includes comprehensive behavioral assessments, detailed linguistic annotations with time-aligned speech segmentation and prosodic features, and natural language processing-derived word predictors. This resource addresses a significant gap in language neuroscience by providing robust neuroimaging data from an underrepresented demographic and linguistic population, enabling investigation of spatiotemporal dynamics of language comprehension in healthy aging.
methods_description: Participants (52 older Cantonese speakers, 40 females, mean age 69.12 years) completed separate fMRI and EEG sessions with a minimum two-week interval. During both sessions, participants listened to four sections of 'The Little Prince' in Cantonese (~20 minutes total) while comprehension was assessed via 20 yes/no questions. fMRI acquisition included T1-weighted structural scans and task-based fMRI during listening, followed by resting-state fMRI. EEG was recorded continuously during all four listening runs. Cognitive assessments (digit span, picture naming, verbal fluency, Flanker task) and questionnaires (Language History Questionnaire 3, Lifetime of Experiences Questionnaire) were administered. MRI data were collected at the University Research Facility in Behavioral and Systems Neuroscience; EEG data at the Speech and Language Sciences Laboratory, both at Hong Kong Polytechnic University.
